title: Replay window accepts first packet unconditionally, no epoch check
description: In rust/lichen-link/src/replay.rs:85-91, the first packet to arrive is always accepted regardless of its sequence number. Combined with the link_layer.rs receive path, if an attacker captures an old frame with epoch=5,seqnum=1000, then the victim reboots (clearing replay state), the attacker can replay that frame. The replay window stores (pubkey,epoch) pairs but doesn't persist across reboots. This is spec-compliant but a footgun for deployments without persistent storage. Document this or add an init method that takes last-known epoch.
close_reason: Fixed: Added epoch validation to ReplayProtector. New set_min_epoch() method allows setting minimum acceptable epoch per peer, which should be called after boot with last-known epoch from persistent storage to prevent cross-boot replay attacks. Added 3 tests and filed 3 P3 codereview findings as child issues.
